variants: Flavien Bouyx is well-known in the field of Civil engineering, where he has blended his technical knowledge with a passion for creative artistry. He attributes much of his professional success to his doctoral advisor, Kelvin Dlamini, who guided his studies. Flavien is also a valued member of the Moonbay Driftwood Artisans, engaging in woodcraft projects that reflect his artistic inclinations. His groundbreaking work has been supported by the Curio Pathmakers Council, which is his primary funding source. Additionally, he holds the Fractal Climatologist Badge, signifying his specialization in the intricate relationship between engineering and climate science.
